Title: GhGLDH35A gene-mediated ROS homeostasis and stomatal movement via the ascorbic acid pathway confers alkaline stress tolerance

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Gossypium hirsutum; Arabidopsis thaliana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Zhong 9807; WT and OE3/OE6/OE7 Arabidopsis lines; INVSc1
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S constitutive promoter in Arabidopsis overexpression; GAL1-based inducible pYES2 system in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant/redox metabolite that scavenges ROS, supports photosynthesis, and contributes to alkaline stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal mitochondrial step of the L-galactose/AsA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GLDH; FAD-binding 4 domain; ALO domain; VAO flavoprotein-related
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in leaves, followed by roots, lower in stems; also relatively high in roots, stems, leaves and seeds; strongly induced by alkaline stress and reduced in darkness
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In cotton, strongly induced by alkaline stress in roots, stems and leaves, with transcript abundance maxima in root and leaf from RNA-seq; in transgenic Arabidopsis expressed in OE lines under CaMV35S
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Alkaline stress, mainly NaHCO3 (100 mM in cotton seedling assays; 5, 100, 200 mM in Arabidopsis assays); also responsive to cold, heat, salt, PEG/drought-related stress; light vs dark conditions
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidation of L-galactono-1,4-lactone to ascorbic acid in mitochondria
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported; paper presents GhGLDH35A as crucial for AsA synthesis and alkaline stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ascorbic acid accumulation, maintains ROS homeostasis, enhances antioxidant capacity, promotes stomatal closure under alkaline stress, preserves chlorophyll/photosynthesis, and improves alkaline stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling; subcellular localization in Nicotiana benthamiana; VIGS silencing in cotton; heterologous overexpression in Arabidopsis; yeast complementation/heterologous expression; GLDH activity assay; AsA/DHA quantification; ROS staining; stomatal aperture measurement; chlorophyll and photosynthesis assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhGLDH35A was the most strongly induced GLDH family member under alkaline stress and also responded to multiple abiotic stresses. 2. GhGLDH35A localizes to mitochondria and encodes the terminal AsA biosynthetic enzyme GLDH. 3. Silencing GhGLDH35A in cotton decreased GLDH activity, AsA and DHA levels, antioxidant enzyme activities, chlorophyll content, and stomatal closure, while increasing ROS, ion leakage, MDA, and alkaline sensitivity. 4. Overexpression of GhGLDH35A in Arabidopsis increased GLDH activity, AsA/DHA accumulation, APX and DHAR activities, chlorophyll retention, stomatal closure, root growth/fresh weight, and alkaline tolerance while reducing ROS. 5. Expression of GhGLDH35A in yeast also improved tolerance to NaHCO3 stress.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth penalty under normal conditions in Arabidopsis OE lines; possible inhibition of stomatal aperture under stress may affect gas exchange, but net effect improved stress performance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for breeding and engineering alkaline-tolerant cotton; suitable for overexpression or CRISPR/Cas-based improvement and as a molecular marker for alkali-tolerant germplasm screening
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper also proposes possible dual mitochondrial role in AsA synthesis and respiratory complex I assembly/chaperone function, but the latter was not directly tested in this study
